blocks.text: DTC P0651: Sensor power supply B circuit failure

The PGM-FI ECU has a built-in 5V power monitor for sensors to supply 5V power to each sensor, and supplies stable power to each sensor as a sensor reference power supply. The power supply voltage to the sensors is read by the CPU (A/D input), and if the sensor power supply voltage remains outside the reference range for a certain period of time, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores a DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: On
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
Ignition switch/ ENGINE START STOP switch

blocks.text: The sensor power supply voltage remains above 5.2V or below 4.5V for 2.0 seconds or longer.

items.text: Short-circuit in the power supply of the code (VCC line) between the PGM-FI ECU and the throttle position sensor
Short circuit in power supply between PGM-FI ECU and secondary gear speed sensor cord (VCC line)
Power short circuit in the cord (VCC line) between the PGM-FI ECU and the driven pulley oil pressure sensor
Short circuit of power supply between PGM-FI ECU and torque converter turbine speed sensor cord (VCC line)
Short-circuit in the power supply cord (VCC line) between the PGM-FI ECU and the accelerator pedal position sensor.
Power short circuit in the cord (VCC line) between the PGM-FI ECU and the EGR valve lift sensor*. 1
Power ground fault in the cord (VCC line) between the PGM-FI ECU and the throttle position sensor
Power ground fault in cord (VCC line) between PGM-FI ECU and secondary gear speed sensor
Power ground fault in cord (VCC line) between PGM-FI ECU and driven pulley oil pressure sensor
Power ground fault in cord (VCC line) between PGM-FI ECU and torque converter turbine speed sensor
Power ground fault in the cord (VCC line) between the PGM-FI ECU and the accelerator pedal position sensor
Power ground fault in cord (VCC line) between PGM-FI ECU and EGR valve lift sensor*. 1

blocks.text: *1: Cars not equipped with a turbo charger
If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
